stemHtml: Which of the following statements is correct about hail?

options.a: Hail is formed when saturated air rises and forms cloud. The latent heat that is dissipated causes the cloud droplets to freeze as they form.
options.b: Hailstones can be so large that they can inflict significant damage to airframes even at low speed.
options.c: Hailstones only reach the ground when the air temperature at the surface is below 0°C.
options.d: Hail is often encountered falling from cirrus or cirrostratus clouds at a warm front.
